trm:term.trm:name: Combined Document
trm:term.trm:definition: An assembled XML document containing content elements from all layer files of a specification, wrapped in a cmb:spec element. The validator unwraps children from each file's clayers root and validates the combined result against a dynamically generated combined schema, which enforces both structural validity per layer and cross-layer referential integrity via xs:unique and xs:keyref.

trm:term.trm:name: Cross-Layer Reference
trm:term.trm:definition: An attribute in one layer that points to a node ID defined in a different layer. Since XSD xs:IDREF is single-document scoped, these use xs:string in individual schemas. The combined schema enforces resolution via xs:keyref against the unified xs:unique constraint on all @id attributes.

pr:section.pr:title: Cross-Layer Referential Integrity
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How references between layers are validated.
pr:section.pr:p: XSD xs:IDREF is strictly single-document scoped. Since each layer is a separate document, cross-layer references use xs:string in their individual schemas. Referential integrity is enforced at the combined document level.

The validator assembles a combined document from all layer files listed in the index. At validation time, the tool dynamically generates a combined schema by scanning layer schemas for appinfo annotations. The generated schema defines:

pr:ul.pr:li: An xs:unique constraint collecting all @id attributes across all layers into a unified ID space.
xs:keyref constraints for every reference attribute type, declared via spec:keyref annotations in each layer schema's appinfo.

pr:section.pr:note: xs:unique is used instead of xs:key because the selector .//* matches all descendant elements, including those without @id. xs:unique skips elements missing the field; xs:key would error on them. xs:keyref works against both.

pr:section.pr:title: Validation
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How the validator discovers, assembles, and validates specifications.
pr:section.pr:p: The CLI tool validates specifications in a single pass by assembling and validating the combined document. The combined schema is generated dynamically from layer schema annotations, so structural validation and referential integrity checking happen together.

pr:step.pr:cmd: Discover XML files from the given path.
pr:step.pr:info: The input may be a single file or a directory.

pr:step.pr:cmd: Resolve each file to its owning index.
pr:step.pr:info: Index files contain manifest entries as children. Non-index files use the root index attribute to locate the manifest.

pr:step.pr:stepresult: The validator has the complete list of layer files for each spec.

pr:step.pr:info: Create a cmb:spec wrapper element and unwrap children from each layer file's clayers root.
Scan all layer .xsd files for spec:content-element appinfo markers and spec:keyref annotations.
pr:step.pr:stepresult: The schema contains both the layer choice group and referential integrity constraints.

pr:step.pr:cmd: Validate the combined document against the generated schema.

pr:step.pr:stepresult: Each spec reports pass or fail with error details.

diag:issue.diag:title: Reference validation fails
diag:issue.diag:condition: The validator reports that a key reference does not resolve.
diag:issue.diag:cause: A layer contains a reference attribute whose value does not match any node ID in the combined document.
diag:issue.diag:remedy: Check the spelling of the reference value and confirm the target file is listed in the index manifest.

pr:section.pr:p: Namespace discovery is dynamic. The tool scans all .xsd files in the schema directory at startup to build prefix-to-URI mappings, rather than hardcoding them. Layer schemas are the source of truth for namespaces.

org:concept.org:purpose: Understanding how references between separate XML documents are validated via the combined schema.

org:task.org:purpose: The validator discovers, assembles, and validates specifications.

rel:relation.rel:note: Cross-layer integrity is the mechanism that makes the layered architecture's shared ID space actually work.
The validation process centers on assembling and validating the combined document.
The validator enforces cross-layer referential integrity as part of its single-pass validation.
The validator uses the index to discover layer files and assemble the combined document.
The diagnostic issue describes a failed reference-validation condition.
The issue is represented in the diagnostic layer.

art:mapping.art:note: clayers-cli implements the full discovery, assembly, and validation flow described in the spec.
Keyref collection from appinfo and keyref generation + xs:unique/xs:keyref output implement cross-layer referential integrity.
Content element collection and combined schema assembly for cross-layer referential integrity.
assemble_combined() creates the combined document by unwrapping children from each file's clayers root into a cmb:spec wrapper.
check_references() and collect_all_ids() implement cross-layer reference checking and ID collection.
check_relation_refs() validates cross-layer reference resolution via xs:keyref constraints.
find_xml_files() discovers XML files from the given path.
is_index_file() + resolve_index() (207-227) and find_index_files() (598-611) implement index resolution.
discover_spec_files() reads the file manifest from index files.
assemble_combined() creates the combined document wrapper.
Acyclic type discovery and collection functions for combined schema generation.
Content element discovery and collection from XSD appinfo annotations for combined schema generation.
validate command: schema validation + iter_errors loop.

rel:relation.rel:note: Step: discover XML files from path.
Step: check if index or resolve index attribute.
Step: read file manifest from index.
Step: validate combined document against schema.

spec:clayers.llm:schema: The combined schema is not a layer file but a validation wrapper, generated dynamically at validation time. The validator scans all layer .xsd files for spec:content-element appinfo markers (to build the choice group) and spec:keyref annotations (to emit keyref constraints). It imports all layer schemas for structural validation and defines xs:unique on all @id attributes plus xs:keyref for every cross-layer reference type. No static combined.xsd file exists: adding a new layer only requires the layer schema itself.
spec:clayers.llm:node: Explains how cross-document references work despite XSD IDREF being single-document scoped. The solution: assemble a combined document and use xs:unique + xs:keyref. This is the most technically subtle part of the design.

The step-by-step process the validator follows: discover XML files, resolve indexes, unwrap children from each file's clayers root, assemble combined document, generate the combined schema from layer appinfo annotations, validate. This maps directly to the clayers-cli implementation.
